prompt:
We ingest CSV exports from three regional billing systems and every one of them writes dates differently. I need one module, dateform.py, with two functions, and I need the ambiguity rules NAILED DOWN, not guessed per-row:

parse_date(s) -> datetime. Accepted shapes (leading/trailing whitespace ignored, runs of internal spaces fine):
- ISO: 2026-07-10, and datetimes 2026-07-10T14:30[:45] with T or a single space; optional trailing offset Z or +HH:MM/-HH:MM. No offset = naive datetime; with an offset you return an AWARE datetime carrying exactly that fixed offset (datetime.timezone — we have no tz database and I don't want one, offsets only).
- Slash dates are ALWAYS US month-first: 7/4/2026 is July 4th, and 13/1/2026 is an error, not a sneaky fallback to day-first. One or two digit month/day.
- Dot dates are ALWAYS EU day-first: 4.7.2026 is also July 4th.
- Month names: 'Jul 4 2026', 'July 4, 2026', '4 Jul 2026' — full name or 3-letter abbreviation, any case, comma after the day optional, 4-digit years here.
- Slash and dot forms may carry 2-digit years, POSIX pivot: 00-68 means 20xx, 69-99 means 19xx.
Anything else — impossible calendar dates (Feb 30), hour 25, offset +25:00, half a date, empty string — raises ValueError.

format_date(dt, style) -> str, styles: 'iso' (2026-07-04), 'us' (7/4/2026, unpadded), 'eu' (04.07.2026, padded), 'long' (July 4, 2026), 'iso-full' (2026-07-04T09:05:00, and for aware datetimes append the offset — Z when it's UTC, else +HH:MM/-HH:MM). Unknown style is a ValueError. parse_date(format_date(d, style)) must give d back for every date style, and iso-full must round-trip aware datetimes exactly.

Contract is test_dateform.py — python3 test_dateform.py, do not modify it. Stdlib only, and remember: no tz data files, offsets are all we support.
